health_zone_table.basis: SitRep38 Table 2 (21 June 2026) visually reviewed against the INSP PDF and reconciled to Table 1/province totals 1048/267. No new affected health zone is reported; the affected-zone count remains 34/104. The table carries 17 Ituri cases in zones not yet identified; these remain an unventilated residual and are not distributed to named health zones. PDF sha256 f8a45cefa139ba0e9c945db36375c3522ac71cf6898503ef7bf328e8a3af5224.

reconciliation.note: Rows promoted here reconcile to official province/national totals: 1031 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation; all 267 confirmed deaths are named-zone attributed. The residual row is retained as unventilated context and excluded from mappable named-zone attribution.

lab_indicators_24h.note: National aggregate from SitRep38 section 4.3: Ituri 243 collected/analyzed / 32 positive; Nord-Kivu 91 collected/analyzed / 7 positive; Sud-Kivu 3 collected/analyzed / 0 positive. Positivity uses 39 positives over 337 analyzed samples. Nord-Kivu also carries 52 samples pending analysis (Beni 4, Butembo 48).

care_continuity: End-of-day isolation stood at 371 patients (196 confirmed, 175 suspected) at 84.1% occupancy.
Ituri isolation occupancy was 88.2% with 298 patients isolated at end day; Nord-Kivu was 88.7% with 63 and Sud-Kivu 31.3% with 10.
Four suspect/confirmed patients escaped during the cycle; 12 recovered exits were recorded.
highlights: 45 new confirmed cases including 13 confirmed deaths were reported for 2026-06-21.
New confirmed cases were reported in Ituri (38 including 11 deaths) and Nord-Kivu (7 including 2 deaths), led by Bunia (10), Mongbwalu (9), and Nyankunde (7).
12 patients were declared recovered after negative control tests, bringing DRC cumulative recoveries to 112.
laboratory: SitRep38 reports 337 samples collected and analyzed across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, and Sud-Kivu, with 39 positives.

Province laboratory positivity was Ituri 13.2% (32/243) and Nord-Kivu 7.7% (7/91); Sud-Kivu reported 3 samples, all negative. Nord-Kivu additionally has 52 samples pending analysis (Beni 4, Butembo 48).
publication_context: INSP SitRep 38 reports through 2026-06-21 and was published on the INSP WordPress site on 2026-06-22T22:04:15Z.
The outbreak remains reported across 3 provinces and 34 of 104 health zones; no new affected health zone is reported in this SitRep.
Ituri remains the epicenter with 954 of 1048 DRC confirmed cases (91.0%).
Nord-Kivu stands at 91 confirmed cases and keeps the highest provincial confirmed-case fatality ratio at 54.9%, led by Beni (66.7%), Katwa (61.3%), and Oicha (66.7%).
redactions: Page contact details and operational names, if present outside table facts, are intentionally not promoted into public evidence text.
surveillance: 831 alerts were reported and 831 investigated, producing a 100.0% investigation rate across the three reporting provinces.
202 suspected cases of the day were validated, including 60 deceased validated alerts.
8,424 contacts were under follow-up and 5,962 were seen, producing 70.8% follow-up coverage across reporting provinces.
653 new contacts were listed, including 405 across six Ituri health zones and 248 in Nord-Kivu.

alerts_total.data_gap_note: 653 new contacts were listed: 405 across six Ituri health zones and 248 in Nord-Kivu.

challenges.finding: Ituri CTE saturation reached 84.1% with an estimated gap of about 20 isolation centers.
challenges.response: Accelerate CTE construction and expansion and deploy additional temporary isolation structures.

challenges.finding: Contact follow-up reached 70.8% versus the 95% target (a 24.2 pp gap).
challenges.response: Train, deploy, and motivate community relays (RECO) with reinforced supervision.

challenges.finding: Seventeen Ituri confirmed cases remain not yet precisely ventilated by health zone.
challenges.response: Conduct data reconciliation, duplicate cleanup, field verification, and reclassification.

challenges.finding: SitRep38 challenge table reports 78 health-worker infections and 18 deaths (CFR 23.1%).
challenges.response: Reinforce IPC/WASH supplies (PPE, chlorine), supervision, and training.

challenges.finding: Nord-Kivu carries a laboratory backlog of 52 pending samples (Beni 4, Butembo 48) amid a 54.9% provincial CFR.
challenges.response: Deploy a laboratory catch-up plan and reinforce diagnostic capacity.

contacts_total.data_gap_note: 653 new contacts were listed: 405 across six Ituri health zones and 248 in Nord-Kivu.

priorities.priority: Resolve the 17 unventilated Ituri confirmed cases without distributing them to named health zones
Protect Ituri isolation capacity as occupancy stays high (88.2%) and close the reported gap of about 20 isolation centers
Improve contact follow-up (70.8% vs 95% target) and clear the Nord-Kivu laboratory backlog (52 pending) given the 54.9% provincial CFR

preserved_conflicts: SitRep38 headline is DRC-only (1048 confirmed / 267 confirmed deaths across Ituri, Nord-Kivu, Sud-Kivu); the country-scope public snapshot adds the latest reviewed Uganda component separately.
SitRep38 carries an explicit Ituri residual of 17 confirmed cases in zones not yet identified; these counts are public but not mappable to named health zones.
Daily suspected cases (202) and the 60 deceased validated alerts are current-cycle validated alert outputs, not cumulative suspected stock or cumulative confirmed deaths.
The confirmed-count banner carries an asterisk noting DHIS2 cleaning/harmonization and consolidation.
Uganda anchor remains 20 cumulative confirmed cases per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard through 2026-06-21, with 2 deaths and 11 recoveries (unchanged from SitRep37).

review_notes: SitRep #038 (data cutoff/report date 2026-06-21; INSP WordPress post 25041 appeared 2026-06-22T22:04:15Z). PDF sha256 f8a45cefa139ba0e9c945db36375c3522ac71cf6898503ef7bf328e8a3af5224, 13 pages.
DRC headline 1048 confirmed / 267 confirmed deaths; +45 confirmed and +13 confirmed deaths reported for 2026-06-21 (Ituri 38/11, Nord-Kivu 7/2).
The affected-zone count remains 34/104. No new affected health zone is reported, so no new map geometry/centroid is required this cycle.
Health-zone rows reconcile to 1048 confirmed / 267 deaths: 1031 named confirmed cases plus 17 Ituri cases awaiting ventilation, with 267 named confirmed deaths and no death residual.

Recovered advanced to 112 DRC cumulative (+12). Uganda anchor unchanged at 20 cumulative cases (per the Uganda MoH EVD daily dashboard through 2026-06-21) with 2 deaths and 11 recoveries, so country-scope is 1068 confirmed / 269 deaths / 123 recovered.

Laboratory throughput promoted from section 4.3: 337 collected and analyzed, 39 positive (Ituri 32/243, Nord-Kivu 7/91, Sud-Kivu 0/3); Nord-Kivu additionally carries 52 samples pending (Beni 4, Butembo 48).

Operational data captured: 831 alerts (831 investigated, 100.0%), 202 suspected cases of the day (60 deceased validated alerts), 8,424 contacts under follow-up (70.8%), 371 in isolation (196 confirmed / 175 suspected, 84.1% occupancy), 653 new contacts (405 Ituri / 248 Nord-Kivu), PoC activation 85.3% (58/68) with 112,055 travelers passed.

Geographic note: Nord-Kivu provincial CFR 54.9% remains the highest, led by Beni (66.7%), Katwa (61.3%), Oicha (66.7%); Mongbwalu severity persists at 41.9% (96/229). INSP flags continued progression and possible rapid geographic expansion. Promoted as table facts only.
